items.title: Indonesia begins enforcing social media ban for children under-16s
items.standfirst: Indonesia began enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16 on Saturday, after a minister warned digital platforms there was "no room for compromise." he Southeast Asian nation announced the ban this month citing threats from online pornography, cyberbullying and internet addiction, as concerns grow globally over the impact of social media on children's wellbeing.
items.content: Indonesia began enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16 on Saturday, after a minister warned digital platforms there was "no room for compromise." he Southeast Asian nation announced the ban this month citing threats from online pornography, cyberbullying and internet addiction, as concerns grow globally over the impact of social media on children's wellbeing.

items.title: Indonesia Starts First Southeast Asia Social Media Ban for Kids
items.standfirst: Indonesia began enforcing restrictions on social media accounts for children under 16 on Saturday, becoming the first country in Southeast Asia to adopt nationwide curbs as governments worldwide take a tougher approach to limit harmful online content.
items.content: Indonesia began enforcing restrictions on social media accounts for children under 16 on Saturday, becoming the first country in Southeast Asia to adopt nationwide curbs as governments worldwide take a tougher approach to limit harmful online content.

items.title: Senegal celebrate AFCON win at Stade de France after being stripped of title
items.standfirst: Senegal on Saturday paraded the Africa Cup of Nations trophy they have been ordered to return ahead of a friendly against Peru at the Stade de France in Paris. The Confederation of African Football (CAF) on March 17 reversed Senegal's 1-0 final victory over Morocco in Rabat on January 18 and awarded a 3-0 victory to the hosts.
items.content: Senegal on Saturday paraded the Africa Cup of Nations trophy they have been ordered to return ahead of a friendly against Peru at the Stade de France in Paris. items.title: Senegal parades AFCON trophy at match in France despite being stripped of title
items.standfirst: Senegal on Saturday paraded the Africa Cup of Nations trophy in Saint-Denis's Stade de France despite an unprecedented decision by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) in January to strip the title and hand it to Morocco. The country's football federation lodged an appeal against the ruling with the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) on Wednesday.
items.content: Senegal on Saturday paraded the Africa Cup of Nations trophy in Saint-Denis's Stade de France despite an unprecedented decision by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) in January to strip the title and hand it to Morocco. The campaign aims to deliver 2.5m jabs per week
Tens of thousands of suspected cases have been reported
The government is targeting massive transmission
The response to the campaign has been encouraging

clusters.title: Iran-backed hackers breach FBI director Kash Patel's personal emails
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran-backed hackers breach FBI director's emails

items.title: Iran-backed hackers breach FBI director Kash Patel's personal emails
items.standfirst: A hacker group shares Patel's purported resume and photos - information, the FBI says, is "historical in nature".
items.content: A hacker group shares Patel's purported resume and photos - information, the FBI says, is "historical in nature".

items.title: FBI director Kash Patel hacked as personal photos appear online
items.standfirst: Pro-Iranian hackers say they've infiltrated the account of FBI director Kash Patel after posting his personal photos and documents online.
items.content: Pro-Iranian hackers say they've infiltrated the account of FBI director Kash Patel after posting his personal photos and documents online.

ai_summary: Iran-backed hackers breached the FBI director's emails
The hackers shared personal photos and documents
The FBI says the information is historical in nature
The hackers are pro-Iranian
The breach is a security concern
